name: Week 2 Pull (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)
description: Same core lifts, one key swap: DB rows replace barbell rows (you skipped BB rows W1 because the gym was packed — DB rows need less space and let you go heavier per side). Added a 4th set of pull-ups since you hit 3x8.

exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, warm up upper back
Decompress spine, activate grip and lats
You hit 3x8 in W1 — now do 4 sets. If you can't hit 8 on set 4, that's fine. Get the volume in.
NEW — replaces barbell row. One arm at a time, brace on bench. Pull elbow past ribs, squeeze at top. Needs less space than a barbell.
W1 working weight: 70lbs. Lean back slightly, pull to upper chest.
W1 working weight: 140lbs. Chest up, pull to belly button.
W1 working weight: 47.5lbs. High cable, rope. Pull to face, externally rotate.
W1 working weight: 42.5lbs. Full extension, squeeze at top.
W1 working weight: 20lbs. Bent over, squeeze rear delts.
W1 working weight: 20lbs. Neutral grip, hits brachialis.

tips: Pull-ups go from 3 sets to 4 — you earned it by hitting 3x8 last week
DB rows replace barbell rows — more practical at Arrilaga and you can go heavier per arm
Everything else stays the same — beat your Week 1 numbers
Rest 3 min between pull-ups, 2 min between rows, 75s between cable isolation
Cable station: swap attachments, don't move. Same efficiency trick as W1.
If a machine is taken, check Training Notes for swap options
Total volume: ~19 working sets for pull muscles (up from 18)
